title: trusted event time
manager: Trusted event-time — available only at the witnessed tier — so a receipt can make a defensible claim about when something happened, not merely that it happened. Time is only as trustworthy as its source; binding trusted time to the witnessed tier (where inputs are already provenance-signed) is what keeps "this ran before the deadline" from being a claim anyone could forge by lying about their own clock.
cs: trusted event-time only at the witnessed tier (W10.6); defensible when-claims; bound to provenance-signed inputs.
